content.content:
1	# Architecture
2	
3	## Overview
4	
5	PopDAM3 is a hybrid cloud/on-premises Digital Asset Manager for licensed consumer-product art. A React/Vite frontend (served via nginx on a Coolify-managed VPS) connects to a Supabase PostgreSQL database and twelve Deno edge functions (project ID `ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`). A Node.js bridge agent running in Docker on a Synology NAS scans the filesystem, generates thumbnails, and pushes them to DigitalOcean Spaces; a separate Windows render agent handles Illustrator files. A persistent Railway cloud worker runs AI tagging, ERP enrichment, and style group rebuilds by polling a job queue stored in the `admin_config` table. The same Docker image serves both PopDAM (`dam.designflow.app`) and PopSG (`sg.designflow.app`); the active mode is determined at runtime by hostname.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Components
10	
11	### Frontend (React/Vite)
12	
13	Located in `src/`. Built with Vite 5, React 18, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn/UI, and TanStack Query 5. Served from an `nginx:1.27-alpine` container (GHCR image `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend`) managed by Coolify (app UUID `qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy`) on VPS `178.156.180.212`. Traefik terminates TLS and routes both production hostnames to the same container.
14	
15	**Dual-mode design:** `src/lib/app-mode.ts` runs mode detection once at module load. Priority order: (1) `?mode=popsg` or `?mode=popdam` query param, persisted to `sessionStorage`; (2) existing `sessionStorage` value; (3) hostname (`sg.*` or `popsg.*` prefix → PopSG; everything else → PopDAM). Exports `APP_MODE`, `IS_POPSG`, `IS_POPDAM`, and `CURRENT_APP`. The Supabase URL and anon key (`ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`) are hardcoded in this file — the Lovable platform overwrites `.env` on every deploy, so environment variables cannot be used. PopSG-specific pages live in `src/pages/popsg/`; the router in `src/App.tsx` guards them with `IS_POPSG`. PopDAM-only tables (`assets`, `style_groups`, `erp_items_current`) are never queried in PopSG mode.
16	
17	**Key areas:**
18	- `src/components/library/` — asset grid, detail panel, filter sidebar (PopDAM only)
19	- `src/components/settings/` — admin UI tabs: bulk ops, ERP, agents, diagnostics
20	- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` — auto-generated by `deploy-supabase.yml` on every deploy; do not edit manually
21	
22	### Supabase (DB + Edge Functions)
23	
24	**Project ID:** `ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb` (host: `ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb.supabase.co`)
25	
26	PostgreSQL with extensions `pg_cron`, `pg_net`, `pg_trgm`, `pgcrypto`, `pgsodium`/Vault. Schema changes are managed via timestamped migration files in `supabase/migrations/` applied through `supabase db push` in CI (`deploy-supabase.yml`). Migration filename timestamps must exactly match the timestamp Supabase records when `apply_migration` MCP is called.
27	
28	Twelve deployed Deno edge functions (see Key Edge Functions section). Functions that require CORS preflight have `verify_jwt = false` in `supabase/config.toml`; JWT verification is done inside the function body. Shared code lives in `supabase/functions/_shared/`.
29	
30	### Bridge Agent
31	
32	Located in `apps/bridge-agent/`. A TypeScript/Node.js process packaged as a Docker image (`ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge`, tag `:stable` for production). Runs in Docker on the Synology NAS via a reference compose file in `deploy/synology/`.
33	
34	**What it does:** registers with `agent-api` using a one-time pairing code, then enters a continuous loop. Receives commands via Supabase Realtime (instant) or heartbeat polling (fallback every 30 s). On a scan command: validates roots → walks the NAS filesystem → computes quick hash (first 64 KB + last 64 KB + file size) → generates thumbnails using MuPDF and Sharp → uploads thumbnails to DigitalOcean Spaces → calls `agent-api` to ingest metadata into Supabase. Also handles PDF text sampling, AI sentinel file detection, PopSG style guide crawls, and self-update.
35	
36	**Communication:** outbound HTTPS only. The cloud never initiates connections to the NAS. Commands are delivered via the heartbeat response payload or Supabase Realtime channel.
37	
38	**Multi-tenant support:** `tenant-supervisor.ts` spawns one child process per tenant when the `TENANTS` env var is set. In the standard single-tenant install this is unused.
39	
40	**Key env vars:** `SUPABASE_URL`, `AGENT_KEY`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (for Realtime), `NAS_CONTAINER_MOUNT_ROOT`, `SCAN_ROOTS`, `DO_SPACES_*`, `POPDAM_CONTAINER_NAME`.
41	
42	### Worker
43	
44	Located in `apps/worker/`. A persistent Node.js process deployed on Railway (auto-deploys from every push to `main`). Uses a service-role Supabase client (`SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`).
45	
46	**What it does:** polls `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS` every 1 s (configurable). Each tick reads the operations JSON blob, detects stale locks, auto-resumes interrupted ops, promotes queued ops into empty lanes with conflict isolation, dispatches batch handlers, and persists state back via the `update_bulk_operation` RPC. There is no HTTP endpoint; all triggering is done by writing to `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS` from the frontend or admin API.
47	
48	**Handlers:** `ai-tagging.ts` (AI tag operations), `erp.ts` (ERP enrichment + AI category classification), `style-groups.ts` (rebuild, reconcile, cleanup), `relink-orphaned.ts`, `tag-propagation.ts`.
49	
50	**Lane isolation:** ops are assigned lanes (`ai-tagging`, `style-groups`, `erp`, `metadata`). Cross-lane conflicts are enforced (e.g., `ai-tag-*` and `rebuild-style-groups` cannot run concurrently). Kill switches stop a lane after 20 identical errors or 80%+ failure rate over 50+ items.
51	
52	### popdam-helper
53	
54	Located in `apps/popdam-helper/`. An Electron desktop application (built with electron-vite, React 18) distributed for Windows (x64) and macOS (arm64 + x64) via GitHub Releases (`popdam-helper-latest` tag), built by `publish-popdam-helper.yml`.
55	
56	**What it does:** provides a GUI for the checkout/checkin workflow. Users check out assets from the DAM to local disk, edit them, then check them back in via Synology WebDAV upload. Watches local files with `chokidar`. Runs a local HTTP server on port 47380 (`GET /status`, `GET /browse?path=X`) for fast directory browsing from the web UI. Credentials (Supabase session + Synology credentials) stored in OS keychain via `safeStorage`. Upload queue persisted to `userData/upload-queue.json` with exponential-backoff retry. Auto-update is wired via `electron-updater` but not yet active — blocked on code signing certificates.
57	
58	---
59	
60	## Data Flow
61	
62	### 1. Asset Ingestion
63	
64	1. Admin triggers a scan from the web UI (Settings → Agents) or the bridge agent receives a `SCAN_REQUEST` from `admin_config`.
65	2. `admin-api` writes the command; the bridge agent picks it up on the next heartbeat or via Supabase Realtime.
66	3. Bridge agent walks the NAS filesystem, hashing files (first/last 64 KB + size). Files already in Supabase with matching hash and path → noop; matching hash but different path → move (preserves tags, writes `asset_path_history`).
67	4. For new/changed files, the bridge agent generates a thumbnail (MuPDF for PDF, Sharp for images, queues to Windows Agent for `.ai`), uploads it to DigitalOcean Spaces bucket `popdam` (CDN: `cdn.designflow.app`), and records the `thumbnail_url`.
68	5. Bridge agent calls `POST /agent/batch-ingest` on `agent-api` with up to 100 files per batch. `agent-api` upserts rows into `assets`, setting `relative_path` (canonical POSIX, no leading slash), `quick_hash`, `modified_at` (filesystem mtime, never server time), `thumbnail_url`, and derived metadata.
69	6. The `trg_compute_primary_sort_tier` trigger assigns a cover tier on every insert/update. The `trg_sync_primary_on_thumbnail` trigger (fires on INSERT and UPDATE) updates `style_groups.primary_asset_id` when a thumbnail is set.
70	7. Bridge agent calls `POST /agent/scan-progress` throughout and reports completion.
71	
72	### 2. ERP Sync
73	
74	1. `erp-sync` edge function is triggered from Settings → ERP Enrichment (full or incremental sync).
75	2. Fetches from `https://api.item.designflow.app/lib/getApiAllItems` (incremental: watermark from `ERP_LAST_SYNC_DATE`; full: all items).
76	3. Normalizes field values through `supabase/functions/_shared/mg-codes.ts` (reverse-lookup: API description → single-char MG code). Unknown descriptions are stored as `null` code + raw value in `raw_mg_fields` jsonb.
77	4. Upserts rows into `erp_items_current` (one row per `external_id`), snapshots to `erp_items_raw`, records run metadata in `erp_sync_runs`.
78	5. The Railway worker's `erp-enrichment` handler runs separately: reads `erp_items_current` in batches of 100, matches style numbers to `style_groups`/`assets`, writes ERP metadata. Guard: only uses `mg_category` to set `product_category` when `erp_updated_at >= 2025-05-10` (pre-cutoff items fall through to AI classification).
79	
80	### 3. Sibling Scan
81	
82	1. Frontend calls `admin-api` route `list-sibling-images` or `ingest-sibling-images`.
83	2. Admin-api writes a `sibling_scan_request_*` row to `admin_config`.
84	3. Bridge agent claims the request via `agent-api` route `claim-sibling-scan`, walks the specified folder looking for sibling JPG/PNG/eligible PDF files alongside existing assets, and reports results through `complete-sibling-scan`.
85	4. `claimed` is a lease. Admin-api expires stale claimed rows after 10 minutes, and agent-api allows the Bridge Agent to reclaim stale claims. Do not remove this; otherwise a Bridge Agent restart or exception can leave the UI waiting forever for the same folder.
86	5. Admin-api handler (`sibling-scan-handlers.ts`) ingests confirmed siblings into `assets`.
87	
88	### 4. AI Classification
89	
90	1. Admin triggers "Classify Now" from Settings → ERP Enrichment → calls `admin-api` route `classify-erp-categories` → writes `erp-classify` op to `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS`.
91	2. Railway worker's `erp-classify` handler reads `erp_items_current` records lacking `product_category`, batches them, and calls OpenRouter (model from `admin_config.AI_TASK_MODELS.text_classification`, default `anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku`, cached 60 s).
92	3. AI returns category + confidence. Items with confidence ≥ 0.65 get status `auto_applied`; below threshold get `pending`.
93	4. Results inserted into `product_category_predictions`.
94	5. Human reviews pending predictions in ERP Items Browser or Review Queue. Approved predictions are pushed to `style_groups.product_category` and `assets.product_category` via "Apply ERP Enrichment" (`erp-enrichment` worker op).
95	
96	---
97	
98	## Key Database Tables
99	
100	| Table | Purpose |
101	|-------|---------|
102	| `assets` | One row per indexed file. Key columns: `relative_path` (POSIX, no leading slash), `quick_hash`, `modified_at` (filesystem mtime, NOT NULL), `thumbnail_url`, `style_group_id`, `primary_sort_tier`, `product_category`, `ai_tagged_at` |
103	| `style_groups` | One row per SKU+folder. Key columns: `sku`, `folder_path`, `primary_asset_id`, `asset_count`, `designer_conflict`, `product_category`, `erp_updated_at` |
104	| `asset_tags` | Normalized tag rows; `source` is `'manual'` or `'ai'` |
105	| `asset_characters` | Character associations for assets |
106	| `asset_path_history` | Records path changes for move detection |
107	| `erp_items_current` | Latest normalized ERP row per `external_id`; `mg01_code`–`mg06_code`, `raw_mg_fields` jsonb |
108	| `erp_items_raw` | Immutable ERP API response snapshots |
109	| `erp_sync_runs` | ERP sync run metadata and status |
110	| `product_category_predictions` | AI classification proposals; statuses: `pending`, `auto_applied`, `approved`, `rejected`, `unclassifiable` |
111	| `admin_config` | Key/value config and lightweight job queue. Keys include `BULK_OPERATIONS`, `SCAN_REQUEST`, `SPACES_CONFIG`, `AI_TASK_MODELS`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `BRIDGE_LATEST_BUILD` |
112	| `agent_registrations` | Registered agents; hashed keys, last heartbeat timestamp |
113	| `agent_pairings` | One-time pairing codes (15-minute TTL) |
114	| `profiles` | User profiles |
115	| `user_roles` | Role assignments |
116	| `invitations` | Pending email invitations |
117	| `app_access` | Controls which app(s) a user can access (`popdam` or `styleguides`) |
118	| `render_queue` | Pending Windows render agent jobs |
119	| `pdf_text_samples` | Extracted text from PDF and AI files (for sentinel detection and AI tagging context) |
120	| `ai_sentinel_cleanup_log` | Log of sentinel `.ai` file cleanup operations (temporary — delete when reconciliation is complete) |
121	| `scanner_ai_ignores` | Files permanently ignored by the scanner (e.g., blank-render AI files); includes `snoozed_until` |
122	| `style_guide_files` | PopSG crawled files; `licensor_name`, `is_active`, `thumbnail_url` |
123	| `style_guide_folders` | DISTINCT folder view for PopSG sidebar |
124	| `style_guide_crawl_runs` | PopSG crawl run metadata |
125	| `style_guide_render_queue` | PopSG Windows render jobs |
126	| `helper_devices` | Registered popdam-helper desktop devices |
127	| `helper_tokens` | Auth tokens for helper app sessions |
128	| `asset_checkouts` | Active and historical checkout records |
129	
130	---
131	
132	## Key Edge Functions
133	
134	| Function | Purpose |
135	|----------|---------|
136	| `admin-api` | Admin control plane (~1,300 lines). Routes for scan management, render queue, agent management, ERP enrichment, tag propagation, PDF backfill, diagnostics, TIFF pipeline, sibling scan, user/invitation management. Auth: user JWT + admin role, or service role key as Bearer. |
137	| `agent-api` | Bridge and Windows agent communication (~2,800 lines). Routes: `register`, `heartbeat`, `ingest`, `batch-ingest`, `scan-progress`, `queue-render`, `claim-render`, `complete-render`, `bootstrap`, `report-path-test`, `report-dir-browse`, bulk PDF text insert, style guide crawl. Auth: `x-agent-key` header. |
138	| `helper-api` | Checkout/checkin workflow for the Electron desktop helper. Auth: user JWT. |
139	| `erp-sync` | Fetches from the DesignFlow ERP API and upserts into `erp_items_current` / `erp_items_raw`. |
140	| `ai-tag` | Legacy AI tagging; currently used mainly for PDF text extraction via Google Gemini direct. |
141	| `authenticate-with-authentik` | Legacy Authentik SSO PKCE flow handler; validates via JWKS, exchanges for Supabase session. Hidden from the login page while Microsoft/Azure is primary. |
142	| `bulk-job-runner` | No-op stub. Returns `{ ok: true, message: "replaced by railway worker" }`. The pg_cron schedule that called this was removed in migration `20260322000000`. |
143	| `sync-external` | Syncs licensors, properties, and characters from external APIs. |
144	| `send-invite-email` | Sends invitation emails via Brevo transactional email. |
145	| `export-sql-dump` | Chunked SQL INSERT export for table data. |
146	| `export-table` | CSV/JSON table data export. |
147	| `export-thumbnail-manifest` | Exports a CSV of asset thumbnail URLs. |
148	| `verify-app-access` | Checks whether a user has access to a given app (`popdam` or `styleguides`). |
149	
150	---
151	
152	## Dual-Mode Design
153	
154	Both `dam.designflow.app` (PopDAM) and `sg.designflow.app` (PopSG) are served by the same Docker container and the same Supabase project (`ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`). Traefik routes both hostnames to the `popdam-frontend` container (Coolify UUID `qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy`). The `sg.designflow.app` routing is configured via a Traefik file provider at `/data/coolify/proxy/dynamic/popdam-sg.yml` on the VPS host, referencing the Traefik service name `https-0-qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy@docker`.
155	
156	At runtime, `src/lib/app-mode.ts` detects the active mode from the hostname and sets `IS_POPSG` / `IS_POPDAM`. Mode differences are entirely in the UI layer:
157	
158	- **PopDAM** (`dam.designflow.app`): full DAM with SKU-based asset library, style groups, ERP integration, AI tagging, filter sidebar, bulk operations.
159	- **PopSG** (`sg.designflow.app`): licensor style guide library with folder-based browsing. Uses `style_guide_files` and `style_guide_folders` instead of `assets` and `style_groups`. No SKUs, no ERP, no PopDAM filter sidebar.
160	
161	PopSG-specific pages are in `src/pages/popsg/`. The router in `src/App.tsx` guards them with `IS_POPSG`. To preview PopSG locally: add `?mode=popsg` to the URL (persisted in `sessionStorage` for the tab).
162	
163	The `supabase-popsg/` directory in the repo root is dead code from an earlier architecture where PopSG had its own Supabase project. It is never deployed from.
164	
165	---
166	
167	## admin_config Pattern
168	
169	`admin_config` is a key/value table in Supabase that serves two distinct roles:
170	
171	**1. Configuration store.** Keys like `SPACES_CONFIG`, `AI_TASK_MODELS`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `POPDAM_MOUNT_PATH`, `POPSG_MOUNT_PATH`, `AGENT_API_KEY` hold runtime configuration. The bridge agent receives a full config sync payload on each heartbeat response (secrets are never included in the heartbeat — `DO_SPACES_KEY` and `DO_SPACES_SECRET` stay in the agent's local `.env`).
172	
173	**2. Lightweight job queue.** The key `BULK_OPERATIONS` holds a JSON object keyed by operation name, each with a status (`queued`, `running`, `interrupted`, `completed`, `failed`, `user_stop`), cursor, and progress counters. The Railway worker polls this key every 1 s. The frontend or `admin-api` writes a `queued` entry to trigger a job; the worker picks it up on the next poll, sets it to `running`, processes batches, and writes progress back after each batch. State transitions are atomic via the `update_bulk_operation` RPC with an optimistic-lock guard (`p_only_if_status`). The `SCAN_REQUEST` and `STYLE_GUIDE_CRAWL_REQUEST` keys work similarly for triggering agent operations.
174	
175	---
176	
177	## External Services
178	
179	| Service | Role |
180	|---------|------|
181	| **Supabase** (`ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`) | PostgreSQL database, Auth, Realtime, Edge Functions runtime (Deno). Single project for both PopDAM and PopSG. |
182	| **DigitalOcean Spaces** | S3-compatible object storage. Bucket `popdam`, region `nyc3`. CDN at `cdn.designflow.app`. Stores all asset thumbnails. Path format: `thumbnails/{asset_id}.jpg`. |
183	| **OpenRouter / Anthropic** | AI inference. OpenRouter (`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`) is the preferred provider; `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY` are fallbacks. Used by the Railway worker for ERP category classification and AI asset tagging. The bridge agent's PDF text extraction uses a vision model via OpenRouter as a last-resort fallback after MuPDF + OCR. |
184	| **Coolify** | Container orchestration on VPS `178.156.180.212`. Owns the `popdam-frontend` container lifecycle, runtime env vars, domain bindings, health checks, and restart policy. CI triggers deploys via the Coolify REST API (`POST /api/v1/deploy?uuid=qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy`). |
185	| **Railway** | Hosts the cloud worker (`apps/worker/`). Auto-deploys from every push to `main`. Worker connects to Supabase directly using a service role key. |
186	| **DesignFlow ERP API** | `https://api.item.designflow.app/lib/getApiAllItems`. Source of ERP item data synced into `erp_items_current`. |
187	| **Brevo** | Transactional email for invitation emails, via the `send-invite-email` edge function. |
188	| **Authentik** | Legacy company SSO provider at `auth.designflow.app`. PKCE flow handled by `authenticate-with-authentik` edge function; login button hidden while Microsoft/Azure is primary. |
189	| **GitHub Container Registry (GHCR)** | Stores Docker images: `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend` (`:latest`, `:<sha>`) and `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge` (`:stable`, `:latest`, `:v{version}`). |
190	| **Synology NAS** | On-premises file storage. Source of all design assets. Accessed only by the bridge agent running in Docker on the NAS itself; never accessed directly by the cloud. |
191

# Architecture

## Overview

PopDAM3 is a hybrid cloud/on-premises Digital Asset Manager for licensed consumer-product art. A React/Vite frontend (served via nginx on a Coolify-managed VPS) connects to a Supabase PostgreSQL database and twelve Deno edge functions (project ID `ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`). A Node.js bridge agent running in Docker on a Synology NAS scans the filesystem, generates thumbnails, and pushes them to DigitalOcean Spaces; a separate Windows render agent handles Illustrator files. A persistent Railway cloud worker runs AI tagging, ERP enrichment, and style group rebuilds by polling a job queue stored in the `admin_config` table. The same Docker image serves both PopDAM (`dam.designflow.app`) and PopSG (`sg.designflow.app`); the active mode is determined at runtime by hostname.

---

## Components

### Frontend (React/Vite)

Located in `src/`. Built with Vite 5, React 18, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn/UI, and TanStack Query 5. Served from an `nginx:1.27-alpine` container (GHCR image `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend`) managed by Coolify (app UUID `qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy`) on VPS `178.156.180.212`. Traefik terminates TLS and routes both production hostnames to the same container.

**Dual-mode design:** `src/lib/app-mode.ts` runs mode detection once at module load. Priority order: (1) `?mode=popsg` or `?mode=popdam` query param, persisted to `sessionStorage`; (2) existing `sessionStorage` value; (3) hostname (`sg.*` or `popsg.*` prefix → PopSG; everything else → PopDAM). Exports `APP_MODE`, `IS_POPSG`, `IS_POPDAM`, and `CURRENT_APP`. The Supabase URL and anon key (`ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`) are hardcoded in this file — the Lovable platform overwrites `.env` on every deploy, so environment variables cannot be used. PopSG-specific pages live in `src/pages/popsg/`; the router in `src/App.tsx` guards them with `IS_POPSG`. PopDAM-only tables (`assets`, `style_groups`, `erp_items_current`) are never queried in PopSG mode.

**Key areas:**
- `src/components/library/` — asset grid, detail panel, filter sidebar (PopDAM only)
- `src/components/settings/` — admin UI tabs: bulk ops, ERP, agents, diagnostics
- `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` — auto-generated by `deploy-supabase.yml` on every deploy; do not edit manually

### Supabase (DB + Edge Functions)

**Project ID:** `ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb` (host: `ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb.supabase.co`)

PostgreSQL with extensions `pg_cron`, `pg_net`, `pg_trgm`, `pgcrypto`, `pgsodium`/Vault. Schema changes are managed via timestamped migration files in `supabase/migrations/` applied through `supabase db push` in CI (`deploy-supabase.yml`). Migration filename timestamps must exactly match the timestamp Supabase records when `apply_migration` MCP is called.

Twelve deployed Deno edge functions (see Key Edge Functions section). Functions that require CORS preflight have `verify_jwt = false` in `supabase/config.toml`; JWT verification is done inside the function body. Shared code lives in `supabase/functions/_shared/`.

### Bridge Agent

Located in `apps/bridge-agent/`. A TypeScript/Node.js process packaged as a Docker image (`ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge`, tag `:stable` for production). Runs in Docker on the Synology NAS via a reference compose file in `deploy/synology/`.

**What it does:** registers with `agent-api` using a one-time pairing code, then enters a continuous loop. Receives commands via Supabase Realtime (instant) or heartbeat polling (fallback every 30 s). On a scan command: validates roots → walks the NAS filesystem → computes quick hash (first 64 KB + last 64 KB + file size) → generates thumbnails using MuPDF and Sharp → uploads thumbnails to DigitalOcean Spaces → calls `agent-api` to ingest metadata into Supabase. Also handles PDF text sampling, AI sentinel file detection, PopSG style guide crawls, and self-update.

**Communication:** outbound HTTPS only. The cloud never initiates connections to the NAS. Commands are delivered via the heartbeat response payload or Supabase Realtime channel.

**Multi-tenant support:** `tenant-supervisor.ts` spawns one child process per tenant when the `TENANTS` env var is set. In the standard single-tenant install this is unused.

**Key env vars:** `SUPABASE_URL`, `AGENT_KEY`, `SUPABASE_ANON_KEY` (for Realtime), `NAS_CONTAINER_MOUNT_ROOT`, `SCAN_ROOTS`, `DO_SPACES_*`, `POPDAM_CONTAINER_NAME`.

### Worker

Located in `apps/worker/`. A persistent Node.js process deployed on Railway (auto-deploys from every push to `main`). Uses a service-role Supabase client (`SUPABASE_URL` + `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`).

**What it does:** polls `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS` every 1 s (configurable). Each tick reads the operations JSON blob, detects stale locks, auto-resumes interrupted ops, promotes queued ops into empty lanes with conflict isolation, dispatches batch handlers, and persists state back via the `update_bulk_operation` RPC. There is no HTTP endpoint; all triggering is done by writing to `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS` from the frontend or admin API.

**Handlers:** `ai-tagging.ts` (AI tag operations), `erp.ts` (ERP enrichment + AI category classification), `style-groups.ts` (rebuild, reconcile, cleanup), `relink-orphaned.ts`, `tag-propagation.ts`.

**Lane isolation:** ops are assigned lanes (`ai-tagging`, `style-groups`, `erp`, `metadata`). Cross-lane conflicts are enforced (e.g., `ai-tag-*` and `rebuild-style-groups` cannot run concurrently). Kill switches stop a lane after 20 identical errors or 80%+ failure rate over 50+ items.

### popdam-helper

Located in `apps/popdam-helper/`. An Electron desktop application (built with electron-vite, React 18) distributed for Windows (x64) and macOS (arm64 + x64) via GitHub Releases (`popdam-helper-latest` tag), built by `publish-popdam-helper.yml`.

**What it does:** provides a GUI for the checkout/checkin workflow. Users check out assets from the DAM to local disk, edit them, then check them back in via Synology WebDAV upload. Watches local files with `chokidar`. Runs a local HTTP server on port 47380 (`GET /status`, `GET /browse?path=X`) for fast directory browsing from the web UI. Credentials (Supabase session + Synology credentials) stored in OS keychain via `safeStorage`. Upload queue persisted to `userData/upload-queue.json` with exponential-backoff retry. Auto-update is wired via `electron-updater` but not yet active — blocked on code signing certificates.

---

## Data Flow

### 1. Asset Ingestion

1. Admin triggers a scan from the web UI (Settings → Agents) or the bridge agent receives a `SCAN_REQUEST` from `admin_config`.
2. `admin-api` writes the command; the bridge agent picks it up on the next heartbeat or via Supabase Realtime.
3. Bridge agent walks the NAS filesystem, hashing files (first/last 64 KB + size). Files already in Supabase with matching hash and path → noop; matching hash but different path → move (preserves tags, writes `asset_path_history`).
4. For new/changed files, the bridge agent generates a thumbnail (MuPDF for PDF, Sharp for images, queues to Windows Agent for `.ai`), uploads it to DigitalOcean Spaces bucket `popdam` (CDN: `cdn.designflow.app`), and records the `thumbnail_url`.
5. Bridge agent calls `POST /agent/batch-ingest` on `agent-api` with up to 100 files per batch. `agent-api` upserts rows into `assets`, setting `relative_path` (canonical POSIX, no leading slash), `quick_hash`, `modified_at` (filesystem mtime, never server time), `thumbnail_url`, and derived metadata.
6. The `trg_compute_primary_sort_tier` trigger assigns a cover tier on every insert/update. The `trg_sync_primary_on_thumbnail` trigger (fires on INSERT and UPDATE) updates `style_groups.primary_asset_id` when a thumbnail is set.
7. Bridge agent calls `POST /agent/scan-progress` throughout and reports completion.

### 2. ERP Sync

1. `erp-sync` edge function is triggered from Settings → ERP Enrichment (full or incremental sync).
2. Fetches from `https://api.item.designflow.app/lib/getApiAllItems` (incremental: watermark from `ERP_LAST_SYNC_DATE`; full: all items).
3. Normalizes field values through `supabase/functions/_shared/mg-codes.ts` (reverse-lookup: API description → single-char MG code). Unknown descriptions are stored as `null` code + raw value in `raw_mg_fields` jsonb.
4. Upserts rows into `erp_items_current` (one row per `external_id`), snapshots to `erp_items_raw`, records run metadata in `erp_sync_runs`.
5. The Railway worker's `erp-enrichment` handler runs separately: reads `erp_items_current` in batches of 100, matches style numbers to `style_groups`/`assets`, writes ERP metadata. Guard: only uses `mg_category` to set `product_category` when `erp_updated_at >= 2025-05-10` (pre-cutoff items fall through to AI classification).

### 3. Sibling Scan

1. Frontend calls `admin-api` route `list-sibling-images` or `ingest-sibling-images`.
2. Admin-api writes a `sibling_scan_request_*` row to `admin_config`.
3. Bridge agent claims the request via `agent-api` route `claim-sibling-scan`, walks the specified folder looking for sibling JPG/PNG/eligible PDF files alongside existing assets, and reports results through `complete-sibling-scan`.
4. `claimed` is a lease. Admin-api expires stale claimed rows after 10 minutes, and agent-api allows the Bridge Agent to reclaim stale claims. Do not remove this; otherwise a Bridge Agent restart or exception can leave the UI waiting forever for the same folder.
5. Admin-api handler (`sibling-scan-handlers.ts`) ingests confirmed siblings into `assets`.

### 4. AI Classification

1. Admin triggers "Classify Now" from Settings → ERP Enrichment → calls `admin-api` route `classify-erp-categories` → writes `erp-classify` op to `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS`.
2. Railway worker's `erp-classify` handler reads `erp_items_current` records lacking `product_category`, batches them, and calls OpenRouter (model from `admin_config.AI_TASK_MODELS.text_classification`, default `anthropic/claude-3.5-haiku`, cached 60 s).
3. AI returns category + confidence. Items with confidence ≥ 0.65 get status `auto_applied`; below threshold get `pending`.
4. Results inserted into `product_category_predictions`.
5. Human reviews pending predictions in ERP Items Browser or Review Queue. Approved predictions are pushed to `style_groups.product_category` and `assets.product_category` via "Apply ERP Enrichment" (`erp-enrichment` worker op).

---

## Key Database Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|-------|---------|
| `assets` | One row per indexed file. Key columns: `relative_path` (POSIX, no leading slash), `quick_hash`, `modified_at` (filesystem mtime, NOT NULL), `thumbnail_url`, `style_group_id`, `primary_sort_tier`, `product_category`, `ai_tagged_at` |
| `style_groups` | One row per SKU+folder. Key columns: `sku`, `folder_path`, `primary_asset_id`, `asset_count`, `designer_conflict`, `product_category`, `erp_updated_at` |
| `asset_tags` | Normalized tag rows; `source` is `'manual'` or `'ai'` |
| `asset_characters` | Character associations for assets |
| `asset_path_history` | Records path changes for move detection |
| `erp_items_current` | Latest normalized ERP row per `external_id`; `mg01_code`–`mg06_code`, `raw_mg_fields` jsonb |
| `erp_items_raw` | Immutable ERP API response snapshots |
| `erp_sync_runs` | ERP sync run metadata and status |
| `product_category_predictions` | AI classification proposals; statuses: `pending`, `auto_applied`, `approved`, `rejected`, `unclassifiable` |
| `admin_config` | Key/value config and lightweight job queue. Keys include `BULK_OPERATIONS`, `SCAN_REQUEST`, `SPACES_CONFIG`, `AI_TASK_MODELS`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `BRIDGE_LATEST_BUILD` |
| `agent_registrations` | Registered agents; hashed keys, last heartbeat timestamp |
| `agent_pairings` | One-time pairing codes (15-minute TTL) |
| `profiles` | User profiles |
| `user_roles` | Role assignments |
| `invitations` | Pending email invitations |
| `app_access` | Controls which app(s) a user can access (`popdam` or `styleguides`) |
| `render_queue` | Pending Windows render agent jobs |
| `pdf_text_samples` | Extracted text from PDF and AI files (for sentinel detection and AI tagging context) |
| `ai_sentinel_cleanup_log` | Log of sentinel `.ai` file cleanup operations (temporary — delete when reconciliation is complete) |
| `scanner_ai_ignores` | Files permanently ignored by the scanner (e.g., blank-render AI files); includes `snoozed_until` |
| `style_guide_files` | PopSG crawled files; `licensor_name`, `is_active`, `thumbnail_url` |
| `style_guide_folders` | DISTINCT folder view for PopSG sidebar |
| `style_guide_crawl_runs` | PopSG crawl run metadata |
| `style_guide_render_queue` | PopSG Windows render jobs |
| `helper_devices` | Registered popdam-helper desktop devices |
| `helper_tokens` | Auth tokens for helper app sessions |
| `asset_checkouts` | Active and historical checkout records |

---

## Key Edge Functions

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `admin-api` | Admin control plane (~1,300 lines). Routes for scan management, render queue, agent management, ERP enrichment, tag propagation, PDF backfill, diagnostics, TIFF pipeline, sibling scan, user/invitation management. Auth: user JWT + admin role, or service role key as Bearer. |
| `agent-api` | Bridge and Windows agent communication (~2,800 lines). Routes: `register`, `heartbeat`, `ingest`, `batch-ingest`, `scan-progress`, `queue-render`, `claim-render`, `complete-render`, `bootstrap`, `report-path-test`, `report-dir-browse`, bulk PDF text insert, style guide crawl. Auth: `x-agent-key` header. |
| `helper-api` | Checkout/checkin workflow for the Electron desktop helper. Auth: user JWT. |
| `erp-sync` | Fetches from the DesignFlow ERP API and upserts into `erp_items_current` / `erp_items_raw`. |
| `ai-tag` | Legacy AI tagging; currently used mainly for PDF text extraction via Google Gemini direct. |
| `authenticate-with-authentik` | Legacy Authentik SSO PKCE flow handler; validates via JWKS, exchanges for Supabase session. Hidden from the login page while Microsoft/Azure is primary. |
| `bulk-job-runner` | No-op stub. Returns `{ ok: true, message: "replaced by railway worker" }`. The pg_cron schedule that called this was removed in migration `20260322000000`. |
| `sync-external` | Syncs licensors, properties, and characters from external APIs. |
| `send-invite-email` | Sends invitation emails via Brevo transactional email. |
| `export-sql-dump` | Chunked SQL INSERT export for table data. |
| `export-table` | CSV/JSON table data export. |
| `export-thumbnail-manifest` | Exports a CSV of asset thumbnail URLs. |
| `verify-app-access` | Checks whether a user has access to a given app (`popdam` or `styleguides`). |

---

## Dual-Mode Design

Both `dam.designflow.app` (PopDAM) and `sg.designflow.app` (PopSG) are served by the same Docker container and the same Supabase project (`ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`). Traefik routes both hostnames to the `popdam-frontend` container (Coolify UUID `qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy`). The `sg.designflow.app` routing is configured via a Traefik file provider at `/data/coolify/proxy/dynamic/popdam-sg.yml` on the VPS host, referencing the Traefik service name `https-0-qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy@docker`.

At runtime, `src/lib/app-mode.ts` detects the active mode from the hostname and sets `IS_POPSG` / `IS_POPDAM`. Mode differences are entirely in the UI layer:

- **PopDAM** (`dam.designflow.app`): full DAM with SKU-based asset library, style groups, ERP integration, AI tagging, filter sidebar, bulk operations.
- **PopSG** (`sg.designflow.app`): licensor style guide library with folder-based browsing. Uses `style_guide_files` and `style_guide_folders` instead of `assets` and `style_groups`. No SKUs, no ERP, no PopDAM filter sidebar.

PopSG-specific pages are in `src/pages/popsg/`. The router in `src/App.tsx` guards them with `IS_POPSG`. To preview PopSG locally: add `?mode=popsg` to the URL (persisted in `sessionStorage` for the tab).

The `supabase-popsg/` directory in the repo root is dead code from an earlier architecture where PopSG had its own Supabase project. It is never deployed from.

---

## admin_config Pattern

`admin_config` is a key/value table in Supabase that serves two distinct roles:

**1. Configuration store.** Keys like `SPACES_CONFIG`, `AI_TASK_MODELS`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `POPDAM_MOUNT_PATH`, `POPSG_MOUNT_PATH`, `AGENT_API_KEY` hold runtime configuration. The bridge agent receives a full config sync payload on each heartbeat response (secrets are never included in the heartbeat — `DO_SPACES_KEY` and `DO_SPACES_SECRET` stay in the agent's local `.env`).

**2. Lightweight job queue.** The key `BULK_OPERATIONS` holds a JSON object keyed by operation name, each with a status (`queued`, `running`, `interrupted`, `completed`, `failed`, `user_stop`), cursor, and progress counters. The Railway worker polls this key every 1 s. The frontend or `admin-api` writes a `queued` entry to trigger a job; the worker picks it up on the next poll, sets it to `running`, processes batches, and writes progress back after each batch. State transitions are atomic via the `update_bulk_operation` RPC with an optimistic-lock guard (`p_only_if_status`). The `SCAN_REQUEST` and `STYLE_GUIDE_CRAWL_REQUEST` keys work similarly for triggering agent operations.

---

## External Services

| Service | Role |
|---------|------|
| **Supabase** (`ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb`) | PostgreSQL database, Auth, Realtime, Edge Functions runtime (Deno). Single project for both PopDAM and PopSG. |
| **DigitalOcean Spaces** | S3-compatible object storage. Bucket `popdam`, region `nyc3`. CDN at `cdn.designflow.app`. Stores all asset thumbnails. Path format: `thumbnails/{asset_id}.jpg`. |
| **OpenRouter / Anthropic** | AI inference. OpenRouter (`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`) is the preferred provider; `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` and `GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY` are fallbacks. Used by the Railway worker for ERP category classification and AI asset tagging. The bridge agent's PDF text extraction uses a vision model via OpenRouter as a last-resort fallback after MuPDF + OCR. |
| **Coolify** | Container orchestration on VPS `178.156.180.212`. Owns the `popdam-frontend` container lifecycle, runtime env vars, domain bindings, health checks, and restart policy. CI triggers deploys via the Coolify REST API (`POST /api/v1/deploy?uuid=qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy`). |
| **Railway** | Hosts the cloud worker (`apps/worker/`). Auto-deploys from every push to `main`. Worker connects to Supabase directly using a service role key. |
| **DesignFlow ERP API** | `https://api.item.designflow.app/lib/getApiAllItems`. Source of ERP item data synced into `erp_items_current`. |
| **Brevo** | Transactional email for invitation emails, via the `send-invite-email` edge function. |
| **Authentik** | Legacy company SSO provider at `auth.designflow.app`. PKCE flow handled by `authenticate-with-authentik` edge function; login button hidden while Microsoft/Azure is primary. |
| **GitHub Container Registry (GHCR)** | Stores Docker images: `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend` (`:latest`, `:<sha>`) and `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge` (`:stable`, `:latest`, `:v{version}`). |
| **Synology NAS** | On-premises file storage. Source of all design assets. Accessed only by the bridge agent running in Docker on the NAS itself; never accessed directly by the cloud. |

content.text: The lowercase `architecture.md` is substantive (not a stub). I need to compare with the UPPERCASE versions before consolidating safely. Let me read the remaining three.

content.content:
1	# HYBRID SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE (Brain + Muscle)
2	
3	This system is intentionally split so:
4	- the cloud never needs access to your NAS filesystem
5	- the browser never needs VPN routing to the NAS
6	- the NAS worker can run as a reliable "appliance"
7	- DevOps stays invisible for the human admin
8	
9	---
10	
11	## 1) Components
12	
13	### A) Brain (Cloud)
14	
15	Responsibilities:
16	- Web UI (browse/search/filter/tag) — React/Vite served via nginx in Docker on Coolify
17	- Authentication + roles (Microsoft/Azure SSO for internal users; invitation-only for Google/email-password; legacy Authentik retained but hidden)
18	- Admin config + diagnostics
19	- API endpoints for agents and admins (`admin-api`, `agent-api`, `helper-api` Supabase edge functions)
20	- Scheduled maintenance (pg_cron: nightly SG crawl, nightly asset count reconcile, queue/history purge)
21	
22	Runs on managed hosting (Supabase + Coolify on VPS). No SSH, no servers to maintain.
23	
24	### B) Muscle (Bridge Agent on Synology NAS)
25	
26	Responsibilities:
27	- Scan configured roots on disk
28	- Read timestamps from filesystem (mtime + birthtime when available)
29	- Compute quick hash for move detection
30	- Generate thumbnails (PSD via Sharp, AI via mupdf PDF-compat path)
31	- Detect `.ai` sentinel files (no PDF compat) and add to permanent ignore list
32	- Upload thumbnails to DigitalOcean Spaces
33	- Call agent API to ingest/update/move assets
34	- Send scan progress counters and logs
35	- Run PDF text sampling cascade (mupdf → Tesseract OCR → AI vision)
36	- Crawl PopSG style guide directories
37	
38	Runs as a Docker container on Synology.
39	
40	### C) Optional Muscle #2 (Windows Render Agent)
41	
42	Only used when `.ai`/`.psd` thumbnails can't be reliably generated on the NAS, or when higher-quality renders are needed.
43	- Claims render jobs from `render_queue` or `style_guide_render_queue`
44	- Renders via Illustrator API (ExtendScript) for `.ai`, Sharp/Ghostscript/ImageMagick for others
45	- Uploads thumbnail to DigitalOcean Spaces (PopDAM) or Supabase Storage (PopSG)
46	- Reports completion via agent API
47	
48	### D) POP DAM Helper (Desktop App)
49	
50	Electron app for Windows and macOS. Enables the checkout/check-in workflow.
51	
52	- Users click "Check Out & Open" in the web DAM. The web app calls `helper-api` to generate a short-lived `popdam://` token.
53	- The Helper validates the token, downloads the file from the NAS, and opens it in the native app.
54	- On check-in, the Helper uploads the modified file back to Synology and updates the cloud checkout record via `helper-api`.
55	- On the same machine, the `/files` directory browser probes `http://127.0.0.1:47380/status` on page load. If the Helper is running, directory listings go directly to it via `GET /browse?path=...` — no cloud roundtrip.
56	
57	**Local HTTP server (port 47380):**
58	- `GET /status` — `{ ok, version, roots[] }`
59	- `GET /browse?path=X` — directory listing
60	
61	### E) Railway Worker (Bulk Operation Runner)
62	
63	Persistent Node.js process on Railway. Handles all batch operations too long-lived for edge functions.
64	
65	Responsibilities:
66	- AI image tagging (`ai-tag-untagged`, `ai-tag-all`, `ai-tag-groups`)
67	- Style group rebuild, reconcile, cleanup-mega-group-tags, relink-orphaned-assets
68	- Tag propagation (`propagate-group-tags`)
69	- ERP enrichment and ERP AI classification (`erp-enrichment`, `erp-classify`)
70	- Metadata reprocessing, SKU backfill
71	
72	How it works: polls `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS` every 5 seconds. When it finds an operation with `status: "running"`, it claims a batch, processes it, writes progress back, and loops.
73	
74	---
75	
76	## 2) Dual-Mode (PopDAM / PopSG)
77	
78	**One Docker image, one Coolify app, one Supabase project.** Both `dam.designflow.app` and `sg.designflow.app` run from the same container.
79	
80	```
81	window.location.host
82	  → "dam.designflow.app" → APP_MODE = "popdam", IS_POPDAG = true
83	  → "sg.designflow.app"  → APP_MODE = "popsg",  IS_POPSG = true
84	  → localhost with ?mode=popsg → IS_POPSG = true (sessionStorage override)
85	```
86	
87	Mode detection is in `src/lib/app-mode.ts`. The same Supabase URL and anon key are used for both modes — mode controls routing and UI only, not which Supabase project to hit.
88	
89	**PopDAM uses:** `assets`, `style_groups`, `erp_items_current`, `erp_items_raw`, `product_category_predictions`, render queues, the full filter sidebar.
90	
91	**PopSG uses:** `style_guide_files`, `style_guide_crawl_runs`, `style_guide_render_queue`, the folder-tree UI. No SKUs, no ERP, no filter sidebar.
92	
93	Route guards in `src/App.tsx` use `IS_POPSG` to render `PopSGLibraryPage` / `PopSGSettingsPage` instead of the PopDAM equivalents.
94	
95	---
96	
97	## 3) Communication Model (No Inbound NAS Networking)
98	
99	Hard rule: The cloud backend does NOT "reach into" the NAS by IP.
100	
101	**Outbound Only:** The Bridge Agent polls outward to the cloud. The Cloud never initiates a connection to the NAS.
102	
103	Instead:
104	- The cloud sets work flags / queues work (DB)
105	- The Bridge Agent polls outward (HTTPS) to claim work
106	- The Bridge Agent reports progress outward (HTTPS)
107	
108	Tailscale may be used for user remote access to NAS files (Synology Drive) and human admin, but it is NOT required for the core worker-to-cloud workflow.
109	
110	---
111	
112	## 4) API Boundaries
113	
114	Three separate API surfaces:
115	
116	### agent-api (verify_jwt = false)
117	- Auth: `x-agent-key` header (SHA-256 hashed and compared against `agent_registrations.agent_key_hash`)
118	- Routes for: ingest/update/move, progress, heartbeat, claim jobs, complete jobs, style guide crawl
119	- Strict request/response validation (Zod)
120	
121	### admin-api (verify_jwt = false — JWT verified inside function)
122	- Auth: user JWT + admin role check inside function, **or** the Supabase service role key as Bearer token
123	- Routes for: config, invites, diagnostics, key generation, per-batch operations called by the Railway worker
124	- Service role key auth maps to `userId: "system"` — used by the Railway worker for server-to-server calls
125	
126	### helper-api (verify_jwt = true — standard user JWT)
127	- Auth: user JWT (Bearer token) — the Helper authenticates as the logged-in user
128	- Routes for: device registration, token generation, checkout/checkin lifecycle, heartbeat, logs
129	
130	Hard rule: never mix admin + agent routes in one function.
131	
132	### Railway worker (server process, no HTTP listener)
133	- Auth: uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` env var to create a service role Supabase client directly
134	- For operations it cannot execute via DB RPC, it calls `admin-api` with the service role key as Bearer token
135	- Bypasses RLS entirely (service role)
136	
137	---
138	
139	## 5) Security Rules
140	
141	- No endpoint accepts arbitrary shell commands or raw command strings.
142	- Agent keys: store only hashes; raw key shown once on creation; raw key never returned again.
143	- All edge functions use `corsServe()` from `supabase/functions/_shared/http.ts` — validates request `Origin` against allowlist (`*.designflow.app`, `*.lovable.app`, localhost).
144	- The `run-query` action in admin-api restricts queries to `SELECT` only.
145	
146	---
147	
148	## 6) Data Flow: Asset Ingestion
149	
150	```
151	NAS disk (PSD/AI/PDF)
152	  → Bridge Agent scanner.ts (stat, hash, shouldSkipPath)
153	  → Bridge Agent thumbnailer.ts (Sharp/mupdf)
154	  → Bridge Agent uploader.ts (DigitalOcean Spaces)
155	  → agent-api /ingest → assets table (upsert by quick_hash or relative_path)
156	  → DB triggers:
157	      trg_compute_primary_sort_tier (sets primary_sort_tier)
158	      trg_sync_primary_on_thumbnail (updates style_group.primary_asset_id)
159	      trg_refresh_sg_counts_on_asset_change (updates style_group.asset_count)
160	```
161	
162	If thumbnail generation fails:
163	```
164	  → assets.thumbnail_error set
165	  → trg_auto_queue_render inserts render_queue row
166	  → Windows Render Agent claims render_queue job
167	  → Windows Agent renders → uploads → calls complete-render
168	  → assets.thumbnail_url set
169	```
170	
171	---
172	
173	## 7) Data Flow: Style Group Rebuild
174	
175	All triggered from admin UI → Railway worker:
176	
177	```
178	Stage 1: clear_assets    → assets.style_group_id = NULL (in batches)
179	Stage 2: delete_groups   → style_groups rows deleted (in batches)
180	Stage 3: rebuild_assets  → rebuild_style_groups_batch() RPC (extracts SKU, upserts groups, sets style_group_id)
181	Stage 4: finalize_stats  → reconcile_style_group_stats_batch() in batches:
182	    - sub-stage "counts":    asset_count, latest_file_date per group
183	    - sub-stage "primaries": primary_asset_id per group (by primary_sort_tier)
184	```
185	
186	Nightly reconcile (03:45 UTC daily, pg_cron):
187	```
188	refresh_style_group_counts_batch(array_agg(id))  ← all style_groups
189	```
190	This catches any drift that the INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE trigger missed.
191	
192	---
193	
194	## 8) Deployment (Non-Negotiable)
195	
196	The bridge agent is published as a pre-built Docker image to GHCR (`ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge`). NAS install requires only a `.env` file and a three-line `docker-compose.yml`.
197	
198	**Heartbeat Rule:** The cloud API tracks `last_heartbeat` from each agent. If it is older than 2 minutes, the admin UI displays a "Check Connection" warning.
199	
200	**Image Appliance Mode:** The Bridge Agent is a read-only appliance by default. It should never have write permissions to source art folders unless explicitly configured for metadata embedding.
201	
202	---
203	
204	## 9) Golden Rule: File Date Preservation
205	
206	The Bridge Agent must NEVER modify file timestamps (mtime/birthtime) on source art files. Before any file read, record original timestamps; after, verify and restore if changed. If restoration fails, halt processing and report a critical error. See `docs/PROJECT_BIBLE.md` §15.
207

file.content:
# HYBRID SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE (Brain + Muscle)

This system is intentionally split so:
- the cloud never needs access to your NAS filesystem
- the browser never needs VPN routing to the NAS
- the NAS worker can run as a reliable "appliance"
- DevOps stays invisible for the human admin

---

## 1) Components

### A) Brain (Cloud)

Responsibilities:
- Web UI (browse/search/filter/tag) — React/Vite served via nginx in Docker on Coolify
- Authentication + roles (Microsoft/Azure SSO for internal users; invitation-only for Google/email-password; legacy Authentik retained but hidden)
- Admin config + diagnostics
- API endpoints for agents and admins (`admin-api`, `agent-api`, `helper-api` Supabase edge functions)
- Scheduled maintenance (pg_cron: nightly SG crawl, nightly asset count reconcile, queue/history purge)

Runs on managed hosting (Supabase + Coolify on VPS). No SSH, no servers to maintain.

### B) Muscle (Bridge Agent on Synology NAS)

Responsibilities:
- Scan configured roots on disk
- Read timestamps from filesystem (mtime + birthtime when available)
- Compute quick hash for move detection
- Generate thumbnails (PSD via Sharp, AI via mupdf PDF-compat path)
- Detect `.ai` sentinel files (no PDF compat) and add to permanent ignore list
- Upload thumbnails to DigitalOcean Spaces
- Call agent API to ingest/update/move assets
- Send scan progress counters and logs
- Run PDF text sampling cascade (mupdf → Tesseract OCR → AI vision)
- Crawl PopSG style guide directories

Runs as a Docker container on Synology.

### C) Optional Muscle #2 (Windows Render Agent)

Only used when `.ai`/`.psd` thumbnails can't be reliably generated on the NAS, or when higher-quality renders are needed.
- Claims render jobs from `render_queue` or `style_guide_render_queue`
- Renders via Illustrator API (ExtendScript) for `.ai`, Sharp/Ghostscript/ImageMagick for others
- Uploads thumbnail to DigitalOcean Spaces (PopDAM) or Supabase Storage (PopSG)
- Reports completion via agent API

### D) POP DAM Helper (Desktop App)

Electron app for Windows and macOS. Enables the checkout/check-in workflow.

- Users click "Check Out & Open" in the web DAM. The web app calls `helper-api` to generate a short-lived `popdam://` token.
- The Helper validates the token, downloads the file from the NAS, and opens it in the native app.
- On check-in, the Helper uploads the modified file back to Synology and updates the cloud checkout record via `helper-api`.
- On the same machine, the `/files` directory browser probes `http://127.0.0.1:47380/status` on page load. If the Helper is running, directory listings go directly to it via `GET /browse?path=...` — no cloud roundtrip.

**Local HTTP server (port 47380):**
- `GET /status` — `{ ok, version, roots[] }`
- `GET /browse?path=X` — directory listing

### E) Railway Worker (Bulk Operation Runner)

Persistent Node.js process on Railway. Handles all batch operations too long-lived for edge functions.

Responsibilities:
- AI image tagging (`ai-tag-untagged`, `ai-tag-all`, `ai-tag-groups`)
- Style group rebuild, reconcile, cleanup-mega-group-tags, relink-orphaned-assets
- Tag propagation (`propagate-group-tags`)
- ERP enrichment and ERP AI classification (`erp-enrichment`, `erp-classify`)
- Metadata reprocessing, SKU backfill

How it works: polls `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS` every 5 seconds. When it finds an operation with `status: "running"`, it claims a batch, processes it, writes progress back, and loops.

---

## 2) Dual-Mode (PopDAM / PopSG)

**One Docker image, one Coolify app, one Supabase project.** Both `dam.designflow.app` and `sg.designflow.app` run from the same container.

```
window.location.host
  → "dam.designflow.app" → APP_MODE = "popdam", IS_POPDAG = true
  → "sg.designflow.app"  → APP_MODE = "popsg",  IS_POPSG = true
  → localhost with ?mode=popsg → IS_POPSG = true (sessionStorage override)
```

Mode detection is in `src/lib/app-mode.ts`. The same Supabase URL and anon key are used for both modes — mode controls routing and UI only, not which Supabase project to hit.

**PopDAM uses:** `assets`, `style_groups`, `erp_items_current`, `erp_items_raw`, `product_category_predictions`, render queues, the full filter sidebar.

**PopSG uses:** `style_guide_files`, `style_guide_crawl_runs`, `style_guide_render_queue`, the folder-tree UI. No SKUs, no ERP, no filter sidebar.

Route guards in `src/App.tsx` use `IS_POPSG` to render `PopSGLibraryPage` / `PopSGSettingsPage` instead of the PopDAM equivalents.

---

## 3) Communication Model (No Inbound NAS Networking)

Hard rule: The cloud backend does NOT "reach into" the NAS by IP.

**Outbound Only:** The Bridge Agent polls outward to the cloud. The Cloud never initiates a connection to the NAS.

Instead:
- The cloud sets work flags / queues work (DB)
- The Bridge Agent polls outward (HTTPS) to claim work
- The Bridge Agent reports progress outward (HTTPS)

Tailscale may be used for user remote access to NAS files (Synology Drive) and human admin, but it is NOT required for the core worker-to-cloud workflow.

---

## 4) API Boundaries

Three separate API surfaces:

### agent-api (verify_jwt = false)
- Auth: `x-agent-key` header (SHA-256 hashed and compared against `agent_registrations.agent_key_hash`)
- Routes for: ingest/update/move, progress, heartbeat, claim jobs, complete jobs, style guide crawl
- Strict request/response validation (Zod)

### admin-api (verify_jwt = false — JWT verified inside function)
- Auth: user JWT + admin role check inside function, **or** the Supabase service role key as Bearer token
- Routes for: config, invites, diagnostics, key generation, per-batch operations called by the Railway worker
- Service role key auth maps to `userId: "system"` — used by the Railway worker for server-to-server calls

### helper-api (verify_jwt = true — standard user JWT)
- Auth: user JWT (Bearer token) — the Helper authenticates as the logged-in user
- Routes for: device registration, token generation, checkout/checkin lifecycle, heartbeat, logs

Hard rule: never mix admin + agent routes in one function.

### Railway worker (server process, no HTTP listener)
- Auth: uses `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY` env var to create a service role Supabase client directly
- For operations it cannot execute via DB RPC, it calls `admin-api` with the service role key as Bearer token
- Bypasses RLS entirely (service role)

---

## 5) Security Rules

- No endpoint accepts arbitrary shell commands or raw command strings.
- Agent keys: store only hashes; raw key shown once on creation; raw key never returned again.
- All edge functions use `corsServe()` from `supabase/functions/_shared/http.ts` — validates request `Origin` against allowlist (`*.designflow.app`, `*.lovable.app`, localhost).
- The `run-query` action in admin-api restricts queries to `SELECT` only.

---

## 6) Data Flow: Asset Ingestion

```
NAS disk (PSD/AI/PDF)
  → Bridge Agent scanner.ts (stat, hash, shouldSkipPath)
  → Bridge Agent thumbnailer.ts (Sharp/mupdf)
  → Bridge Agent uploader.ts (DigitalOcean Spaces)
  → agent-api /ingest → assets table (upsert by quick_hash or relative_path)
  → DB triggers:
      trg_compute_primary_sort_tier (sets primary_sort_tier)
      trg_sync_primary_on_thumbnail (updates style_group.primary_asset_id)
      trg_refresh_sg_counts_on_asset_change (updates style_group.asset_count)
```

If thumbnail generation fails:
```
  → assets.thumbnail_error set
  → trg_auto_queue_render inserts render_queue row
  → Windows Render Agent claims render_queue job
  → Windows Agent renders → uploads → calls complete-render
  → assets.thumbnail_url set
```

---

## 7) Data Flow: Style Group Rebuild

All triggered from admin UI → Railway worker:

```
Stage 1: clear_assets    → assets.style_group_id = NULL (in batches)
Stage 2: delete_groups   → style_groups rows deleted (in batches)
Stage 3: rebuild_assets  → rebuild_style_groups_batch() RPC (extracts SKU, upserts groups, sets style_group_id)
Stage 4: finalize_stats  → reconcile_style_group_stats_batch() in batches:
    - sub-stage "counts":    asset_count, latest_file_date per group
    - sub-stage "primaries": primary_asset_id per group (by primary_sort_tier)
```

Nightly reconcile (03:45 UTC daily, pg_cron):
```
refresh_style_group_counts_batch(array_agg(id))  ← all style_groups
```
This catches any drift that the INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE trigger missed.

---

## 8) Deployment (Non-Negotiable)

The bridge agent is published as a pre-built Docker image to GHCR (`ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge`). NAS install requires only a `.env` file and a three-line `docker-compose.yml`.

**Heartbeat Rule:** The cloud API tracks `last_heartbeat` from each agent. If it is older than 2 minutes, the admin UI displays a "Check Connection" warning.

**Image Appliance Mode:** The Bridge Agent is a read-only appliance by default. It should never have write permissions to source art folders unless explicitly configured for metadata embedding.

---

## 9) Golden Rule: File Date Preservation

The Bridge Agent must NEVER modify file timestamps (mtime/birthtime) on source art files. Before any file read, record original timestamps; after, verify and restore if changed. If restoration fails, halt processing and report a critical error. See `docs/PROJECT_BIBLE.md` §15.

content.text: Both architecture docs are substantive but differently-focused (lowercase = accurate component/DB/edge inventory with correct `1s` worker poll; UPPERCASE = "Brain/Muscle" principles with a stale `5 seconds` and an `IS_POPDAG` typo). Consolidation is the spec's clearest concrete ask. Let me read the deployment pair too, then merge each pair into its well-referenced canonical filename.

content.content:
1	# Deployment
2	
3	## Deployment Path
4	
5	Every production change follows this path:
6	
7	1. Developer commits and pushes to `main` on both `origin` (harness proxy) and `github` (direct GitHub remote).
8	2. GitHub Actions evaluates path filters and runs the relevant workflow(s).
9	3. For frontend changes: `publish-frontend.yml` builds the React app, builds a Docker image with `Dockerfile.ci`, pushes it to GHCR as `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend:latest` and `:<short-sha>`, then calls the Coolify deploy API.
10	4. Coolify receives the webhook, pulls `:latest` from GHCR, and replaces the running container. No SSH is involved.
11	5. For Supabase changes: `deploy-supabase.yml` runs `supabase db push` (migrations) and/or deploys all edge functions, then auto-generates and commits `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`.
12	6. For Railway worker changes: Railway detects the push to `main` and triggers its own rebuild automatically — no GitHub Actions step required.
13	
14	Both `dam.designflow.app` (PopDAM) and `sg.designflow.app` (PopSG) are served by the same container. Traefik on the VPS routes both hostnames to `popdam-frontend`.
15	
16	---
17	
18	## GitHub Actions Workflows
19	
20	| Name | File | Trigger | What it does |
21	|------|------|---------|-------------|
22	| CI | `ci.yml` | Push or PR to `main` | Lint, test, and build the frontend with Bun. No deployment. |
23	| Publish Frontend Image | `publish-frontend.yml` | Push to `main` touching `src/**`, `public/**`, `index.html`, `package.json`, `package-lock.json`, `vite.config.ts`, `tailwind.config.ts`, `postcss.config.js`, `tsconfig*.json`, `Dockerfile`, `nginx.conf`, or the workflow file | `npm ci` → `vite build` → `docker build -f Dockerfile.ci` → push to GHCR (`:latest` + `:<sha>`) → POST Coolify deploy API |
24	| Deploy Supabase (Edge Functions + Migrations) | `deploy-supabase.yml` | Push to `main` touching `supabase/functions/**`, `supabase/migrations/**`, or the workflow file; also `workflow_dispatch` | `supabase db push` (if migrations changed) → deploy all edge functions except `_shared` → generate TypeScript types → commit types back to `main` with `[skip ci]` |
25	| Edge Functions Format | `edge-functions-format.yml` | Push or PR to `main` touching `supabase/functions/**/*.ts`; also `workflow_dispatch` | Runs `deno fmt` on `supabase/functions/` and commits any formatting changes back |
26	| Publish Bridge Agent | `publish-bridge-agent.yml` | Push to `main` touching `apps/bridge-agent/**` or `packages/path-filters/**`; also tags matching `bridge-v*` | Builds and pushes Docker image `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge` to GHCR with tags `:latest`, `:stable`, `:v{version}`, `:<sha>`; upserts `BRIDGE_LATEST_BUILD` in `admin_config` via Supabase PostgREST |
27	| Publish Windows Agent | `publish-windows-agent.yml` | Push to `main` touching `apps/windows-agent/**` or `packages/path-filters/**` | Builds TypeScript, bundles Node.js runtime, creates NSIS installer and zip artifact, creates versioned GitHub Release and updates `windows-agent-latest` release, POSTs `notify-build` to `agent-api` |
28	| Publish PopDAM Helper | `publish-popdam-helper.yml` | Push to `main` touching `apps/popdam-helper/**`; also `workflow_dispatch` | Builds Electron app for Windows (x64 NSIS installer) and macOS (arm64 + x64 DMG) in parallel, publishes all artifacts to GitHub Release `popdam-helper-latest` |
29	| Deploy PopSG Supabase (Edge Functions) | `deploy-popsg-supabase.yml` | `workflow_dispatch` only | Deploys edge functions from `supabase-popsg/` to the old PopSG Supabase project. **This workflow targets dead code and should not be used.** |
30	
31	**CI path filters:** `publish-frontend.yml` does not trigger on changes to `docs/**` or top-level `.md` files. `deploy-supabase.yml` does not trigger on frontend source changes.
32	
33	---
34	
35	## Docker Image
36	
37	**Frontend image:** `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend`
38	
39	Tags pushed on every qualifying push to `main`:
40	- `:latest` — what Coolify pulls on deploy
41	- `:<git-short-sha>` — immutable tag for rollback (e.g., `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend:a3f2c1d`)
42	
43	**Dockerfile used by CI:** `Dockerfile.ci` (runtime-only nginx stage; the Vite build runs in the GitHub Actions runner before `docker build` is called)
44	
45	**Runtime base image:** `nginx:1.27-alpine`, port 80. Serves the pre-built `dist/` as static files.
46	
47	**Build-time args injected by CI:** `APP_COMMIT` (git short hash), `APP_DATE` (ISO commit date). These are embedded in the bundle via `vite.config.ts` `define:` and are not runtime env vars.
48	
49	**Bridge agent image:** `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge`
50	
51	Tags pushed when `apps/bridge-agent/**` changes:
52	- `:stable` — what the NAS docker-compose and in-app self-update pull
53	- `:latest`
54	- `:v{semver}` (from `apps/bridge-agent/package.json`)
55	- `:<sha>`
56	
57	---
58	
59	## Coolify
60	
61	**How it is triggered:** GitHub Actions POSTs to the Coolify REST API after pushing the image to GHCR:
62	
63	```
64	GET/POST $COOLIFY_URL/api/v1/deploy?uuid=$COOLIFY_APP_UUID&force=false
65	Authorization: Bearer $COOLIFY_TOKEN
66	```
67	
68	Coolify then pulls `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend:latest` and replaces the running container. No SSH. No `docker run` on the server.
69	
70	**App UUID:** `qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy`
71	
72	**Traefik service name:** `https-0-qxj8a0j3tpa9lq4q5rs6pezy@docker` (stable across container redeploys; derived from app UUID)
73	
74	**Coolify URL:** `https://coolify.designflow.app`
75	
76	**VPS host:** `178.156.180.212`
77	
78	**Runtime environment variables** live in Coolify — not in GitHub and not baked into the image. The frontend container is a pure static file server and has no runtime env vars. Runtime configuration for agents (DO Spaces keys, OpenRouter keys, etc.) is stored in the `admin_config` Supabase table and delivered to agents via heartbeat responses. The Railway worker's env vars (`SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`) are set in the Railway dashboard.
79	
80	**What Coolify owns:** container lifecycle, restart policy, health checks, domain bindings. Domain `dam.designflow.app` is routed via Docker labels that Coolify applies. Domain `sg.designflow.app` is routed via a Traefik file provider at `/data/coolify/proxy/dynamic/popdam-sg.yml` on the VPS host (bind-mounted into `coolify-proxy`), using a `@docker` cross-provider service reference to the same `popdam-frontend` container.
81	
82	---
83	
84	## Supabase Deployment
85	
86	**Workflow:** `deploy-supabase.yml`
87	
88	**Supabase project:** `ryltkzzernhwnojzouyb` (popdam-prod), supplied via GitHub secret `EXTERNAL_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID`.
89	
90	**Migrations:** When `supabase/migrations/**` files change in a push, the workflow runs `supabase db push`. This applies any local migration files not yet recorded in the DB's migration history, in timestamp order. The workflow does NOT use `--include-all`. The CI job checks `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` to detect whether migration files changed before running `db push`.
91	
92	**Edge functions:** When `supabase/functions/**` files change, the workflow deploys all subdirectories under `supabase/functions/` except `_shared`, using `supabase functions deploy <name> --no-verify-jwt`. A failure on one function is logged and the loop continues.
93	
94	**TypeScript type generation:** After deploying, the workflow runs `supabase gen types typescript --project-id $SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID` and writes the output to `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts`. If the file changed, it commits it back to `main` with message `chore: auto-generate Supabase types [skip ci]`.
95	
96	**Required GitHub secrets:** `SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN`, `EXTERNAL_SUPABASE_PROJECT_ID`, `EXTERNAL_SUPABASE_DB_PASSWORD`.
97	
98	**Critical constraint:** The local migration filename timestamp must match the timestamp Supabase recorded when the migration was applied. Use `apply_migration` MCP → `list_migrations` MCP → create local file with the exact recorded timestamp. A mismatch causes `supabase db push` to fail in CI. See `CLAUDE.md` for the full discipline.
99	
100	---
101	
102	## Environment Promotion
103	
104	There is no staging environment. There is one production environment.
105	
106	All changes go directly to `main` and are deployed immediately to production. There are no feature branches, no PR-based workflows, and no staging → production promotion step.
107	
108	Railway auto-deploys on every push to `main` regardless of which files changed (Railway does not support path filters). This means every push to `main` triggers a Railway worker rebuild, even if `apps/worker/` was not touched.
109	
110	---
111	
112	## Rollback
113	
114	**Frontend rollback:**
115	
116	1. In the Coolify UI, navigate to the `popdam-frontend` app and open the deployment history.
117	2. Each past deployment corresponds to a `:<sha>` image tag (e.g., `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-frontend:a3f2c1d`).
118	3. Change the image tag in Coolify to the desired `:<sha>` and trigger a redeploy.
119	4. Coolify pulls that specific image and replaces the container.
120	
121	Do not SSH into the server to roll back. Prefer the Coolify UI path.
122	
123	**Supabase rollback:** Supabase migrations are forward-only. There is no automated rollback mechanism. To undo a migration, write a new migration that reverses the change.
124	
125	**Railway worker rollback:** Railway keeps deployment history. Roll back via the Railway dashboard by redeploying a prior build.
126	
127	---
128	
129	## SSH Access Policy
130	
131	SSH access to `178.156.180.212` is **emergency break-glass only**. It is not part of any normal deployment path.
132	
133	Appropriate uses:
134	- Incident debugging when Coolify cannot provide needed logs
135	- Emergency repairs that cannot wait for a CI cycle
136	- One-off diagnostics during an active incident
137	
138	SSH is not appropriate for:
139	- Routine deployments (use GitHub Actions → Coolify API)
140	- Runtime configuration changes (use Coolify UI)
141	- Schema or edge function changes (use `apply_migration` MCP + push to `main`)
142	- Hotfixes (commit to `main` and push; CI deploys in ~2 minutes)
143	
144	Any change made via SSH must be followed immediately by committing a permanent fix to the repo or recording the change in Coolify. The server must never become a hidden source of truth.
145	
146	The `VPS_SSH_KEY` GitHub secret was removed on 2026-05-15. GitHub Actions no longer has SSH access to the VPS.
147	
148	---
149	
150	## Bridge Agent Deployment
151	
152	The bridge agent runs as a Docker container on the Synology NAS, managed by `docker-compose` with the reference file at `deploy/synology/docker-compose.yml`. The image is `ghcr.io/u2giants/popdam-bridge:stable`.
153	
154	**Building and publishing (automated):** `publish-bridge-agent.yml` triggers when `apps/bridge-agent/**` or `packages/path-filters/**` changes. It builds and pushes the image with tags `:stable`, `:latest`, `:v{semver}`, and `:<sha>`. After a successful push, it upserts `BRIDGE_LATEST_BUILD` in the `admin_config` table so the admin UI can show the current build.
155	
156	**Updating on the NAS (two paths):**
157	
158	Primary — in-app self-update: Admin UI → Settings → Agents → Update. This sets an `UPDATE_REQUEST` command in `admin_config`. The agent receives it on the next heartbeat, pulls `:stable`, and recreates its own container. Requires `POPDAM_CONTAINER_NAME` env var to be set in the agent's `.env`; without it, old containers accumulate.
159	
160	Fallback — manual on NAS:
161	```bash
162	sudo docker compose pull
163	sudo docker compose down
164	sudo docker compose up -d
165	```
166	
167	**Versioning:** bump `apps/bridge-agent/package.json` version in the same commit as any bridge agent change (patch/minor/major per impact).
168	
169	---
170	
171	## Worker Deployment
172	
173	The cloud worker (`apps/worker/`) runs on Railway as a persistent Node.js process.
174	
175	**Deploy trigger:** Railway watches `main` and rebuilds on every push — there is no path filter. A push that only touches `src/` still triggers a Railway rebuild of the worker. This is a Railway platform constraint.
176	
177	**No manual step required.** Railway detects the push, rebuilds from `apps/worker/Dockerfile`, and replaces the running container automatically.
178	
179	**Runtime env vars** are set in the Railway dashboard: `SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY`.
180	
181	**The worker is not triggered by HTTP.** It runs a continuous polling loop, reading `admin_config.BULK_OPERATIONS` every 1 second. Admin UI actions write a `queued` entry to that config key; the worker picks it up on the next poll.
182	
183	**Versioning:** bump `apps/worker/package.json` version in the same commit as any worker change.
184
